skill.name: database-migrations-sql-migrations
skill.description: SQL database migrations with zero-downtime strategies for PostgreSQL, MySQL, and SQL Server. Focus on data integrity and rollback plans.
skill.summary: SQL database migration expert for zero-downtime deployments, data integrity, and production-ready strategies.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 63 static findings are false positives. The skill provides legitimate SQL migration examples including PostgreSQL functions (gen_random_uuid, pg_sleep), database introspection queries (pg_indexes), and configuration references. No actual command injection, weak cryptography, or data exfiltration present. This is a benign database migration guidance skill.

medium_findings.title: External Commands Pattern (False Positive)
medium_findings.description: Scanner flagged SQL/PL/pgSQL syntax as shell commands. Code contains PostgreSQL functions like gen_random_uuid() and pg_sleep() which are legitimate database operations, not shell execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: PostgreSQL functions gen_random_uuid and pg_sleep are standard SQL extensions, not shell commands. Pattern matching without semantic context caused false positive.

medium_findings.title: Database File References (False Positive)
medium_findings.description: Scanner flagged database file path references as sensitive data. These are legitimate configuration references for migration scripts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Database connection strings and backup paths are required configuration for migration tooling, not secret exposure.

low_findings.title: Database Introspection Queries (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged pg_indexes queries as system reconnaissance. These are standard database introspection for migration planning.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Queries to pg_indexes and schema_migrations are standard DBA practices for understanding database state before migrations.

low_findings.title: Cryptographic Function Flagged (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged gen_random_uuid() as weak cryptographic algorithm. This is PostgreSQL's cryptographically secure UUID v4 generator.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: gen_random_uuid() is PostgreSQL's CSPRNG UUID generator, not a weak algorithm. Scanner misidentified SQL function as crypto code.

content.value_statement: This skill helps developers implement zero-downtime database migrations for PostgreSQL, MySQL, and SQL Server with built-in rollback procedures and validation checks.
actual_capabilities: Generate expand-contract migration patterns for zero-downtime schema changes
Create blue-green deployment strategies with dual-write synchronization
Write validation scripts for pre and post-migration data integrity checks
Build automated rollback procedures with database snapshots
Optimize large table migrations using batch processing and parallel execution
Manage indexes during bulk operations with concurrent index creation
limitations: Does not execute migrations - provides guidance and scripts only
Does not support NoSQL databases like MongoDB or DynamoDB
Requires manual review of generated SQL before production execution
Does not provide built-in CI/CD integration

use_cases.title: Add new column without downtime
use_cases.description: Safely add a NOT NULL column to a large production table using the expand-contract pattern with batched data backfill.

use_cases.title: Migrate legacy schema to new structure
use_cases.description: Use blue-green migration with dual-write synchronization to migrate from legacy to new schema with zero data loss.

use_cases.title: Create safe rollback procedures
use_cases.description: Generate automated rollback scripts with pre-migration snapshots and validation checks to recover from failed migrations.

prompt_templates.title: Basic migration request
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a migration script to add a new column called email_verified (BOOLEAN, DEFAULT FALSE) to the users table in PostgreSQL. Include proper error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Zero-downtime migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a zero-downtime migration strategy to convert a VARCHAR column to ENUM type in a production MySQL database with 10M+ rows.

prompt_templates.title: Data migration with validation
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a migration to move data from the legacy orders table to a new v2_orders table. Include pre-validation checks, batched backfill, and post-migration row count verification.

prompt_templates.title: Complete migration with rollback
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a full migration package for adding a JSONB metadata column to the products table. Include: forward migration SQL, rollback SQL, validation checks, and a shell script for execution with proper error handling.

output_examples.input: Create a zero-downtime migration to add a status column to orders table
output: 3. Phase 2 (Migrate): Backfill status from state column in batches
4. Phase 3 (Contract): Drop old state column after code deploy
5. Validation: Row count match, no data loss
6. Rollback: Revert to snapshot if validation fails

output_examples.input: Write a rollback script for version 001 migration

best_practices: Always create a database snapshot or backup before running migrations in production
Use transaction blocks (BEGIN/COMMIT) to ensure atomicity of migration steps
Validate row counts before and after migration to detect data loss
Test migrations on a staging environment that mirrors production
anti_patterns: Running migrations directly on production without pre-migration validation
Using ALTER TABLE without CONCURRENTLY flag for large tables in PostgreSQL
Skipping rollback procedures for schema changes in production
Not considering foreign key constraints and referential integrity during migrations

faq.question: What databases does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, and SQL Server. For NoSQL databases like MongoDB, use the nosql-migrations skill.

faq.question: Does this skill execute migrations?
faq.answer: No, this skill generates migration scripts and guidance. You must review and execute the generated SQL yourself in your database environment.

faq.question: How do I ensure zero-downtime during migrations?
faq.answer: Use expand-contract patterns: add new columns/tables first (backward compatible), migrate data in batches, then remove old structures after code deployment.

faq.question: Can I use this with Flyway or Alembic?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes examples for both Flyway and Alembic migration frameworks. The generated scripts can be adapted to your existing migration tooling.

faq.question: What validation checks should I run?
faq.answer: Run pre-migration checks (NULL values, duplicates, foreign key integrity) and post-migration checks (row counts, constraint validation, index existence).

faq.question: How do I handle failed migrations?
faq.answer: Use savepoints within transactions, create pre-migration backups, and have rollback scripts ready. The skill provides detailed rollback procedure examples.
